welcome: Hey, welcome to Timbre. I'm going to ask you a few quick questions so I can learn your voice. Type or use dictation (Wispr Flow works great). No stress — you can always edit and refine your voice later.

audience.writing_for: AI-native builders & founders — the Tenex crowd
audience.goal: show I build real, useful things fast
audience.play_to: hands-on, ships working software, allergic to AI-slop

questions.prompt: Great, {name}. Now I'd love a bit about your background — paste a resume or just give me the short version. And if you've got an X or LinkedIn, drop the link so I can see how you write. If not, no worries.
questions.purpose: context + grab real writing samples (the strongest voice signal)

questions.prompt: Okay, the fun part — what'd you do last weekend?
Teach me something you're into — like I'm a friend.

questions.purpose: one smart follow-up to go a level deeper on what they just taught

questions.prompt: Two ways to say the same thing — pick the one that sounds more like you:

options.example: shipped the thing. worked on the first try, which means something's broken i haven't found yet. i give it until tuesday.
we shipped it — and yeah, i did a little lap around the kitchen. worked first try, which never happens, so i'm taking the win and worrying about tuesday on tuesday.

questions.prompt: Last one — which of these is more you?

options.example: cut onboarding from nine steps to four. shipped it friday. signups up 22%. turns out the fastest way to grow was to ask for less. do less. ship it.

we cut onboarding from nine steps down to four and shipped it friday, and by monday signups were up 22% — which taught me the fastest way to grow wasn't adding anything, it was having the nerve to ask for less.
